topic: trying to cook nan's rice and peas

turns.content: right so I'm trying to cook rice and peas and it's going terribly. my nan makes it perfect every time and I thought how hard can it be. turns out very hard
Ha, rice and peas looks simple but it's one of those dishes where the technique matters more than you'd think. What's going wrong — is it the rice texture, the flavour, or both?

both bruv. the rice is coming out sticky and the flavour is just... flat. like it tastes like rice with some kidney beans in it. when nan makes it the coconut comes through proper and the rice is fluffy and each grain is separate. mine looks like porridge

Okay, a few things that might help:

**For the texture:**
- Wash the rice properly — rinse it 3-4 times until the water runs clear. That gets rid of the excess starch that makes it sticky.
- Use the right rice-to-liquid ratio. For rice and peas with coconut milk, it's usually about 1 cup rice to 1.5 cups liquid total (coconut milk + water combined).
- Don't stir it once it's cooking. Let it steam with the lid on, low heat.

**For the flavour:**
- Use full-fat coconut milk, not light. That's where the richness comes from.
- Add a whole scotch bonnet on top (don't cut it, just place it whole so it gives aroma without crazy heat).
- Thyme — fresh if you can, a few sprigs. Garlic. Allspice (pimento). Those are the backbone.

What are you currently using for seasoning?

I used coconut milk from a tin and I put allspice in but I didn't wash the rice properly tbh I just rinsed it once. and I was stirring it because I was scared it would burn at the bottom 😭 that explains the porridge texture init

Yeah that's your main culprit right there! Stirring releases all the starch and turns it into mush. Once you bring it to a boil and put the lid on, just leave it alone on the lowest heat for about 20 minutes. Trust the process.

For the burning worry — if the heat is low enough it won't burn. And a bit of crust at the bottom (bun bun / pot crust) is actually a good thing in Caribbean cooking. Some people fight over that bit.

Did you use thyme and scotch bonnet?

I used dried thyme because I didn't have fresh. no scotch bonnet because I couldn't find one at Tesco. nan always uses fresh thyme and she gets her scotch bonnets from the Caribbean shop in St Pauls but I was too lazy to go over there ngl

There's your problem — the Caribbean shop trip is not optional! Fresh thyme and scotch bonnet are non-negotiable for proper rice and peas. Dried thyme works in a pinch but it's not the same flavour at all.

Next time: Caribbean shop for scotch bonnets, fresh thyme, and while you're there grab some proper coconut cream (the thick stuff, even better than tinned milk for richness). And pimento seeds if they have them — freshly crushed allspice hits different.

Your nan's been doing this for decades so don't beat yourself up. It takes practice.

icl the Caribbean shop does have way better ingredients. and nan always says 'you can't shortcut good food Marcus' and she's right tbh. I'm gonna try again this weekend and actually go get the proper stuff. also should I soak the kidney beans or is tinned fine?

Your nan is wise. For the beans:

- **Dried beans** (soaked overnight) give the best flavour and a creamier cooking liquid that thickens the rice beautifully. That liquid is part of the magic.
- **Tinned beans** work if you're short on time but rinse and drain them first, then simmer them in the coconut milk for a bit before adding the rice so they absorb flavour.

If you want it to taste like your nan's, she almost certainly uses dried beans. That overnight soak is part of the ritual.

Quick summary for your next attempt: wash rice 4 times, soak dried beans overnight, full-fat coconut milk or cream, fresh thyme, whole scotch bonnet, allspice, garlic, don't stir, low heat, lid on, 20 minutes. You'll get there.

she does use dried beans yeah. soaks them overnight in the pot on the counter. alright I'm committing to the full method this weekend. no shortcuts. if it comes out good I'm sending her a photo and if it comes out bad I'm deleting the evidence lol. safe bruv appreciate the help
Ha, that's the spirit. I'm sure she'd be proud of you for trying either way. Good luck this weekend — trust the process and keep that lid on!
